name: Machiavelli Discourses EN retrieval golden set
source: Niccolo Machiavelli, Discourses on the First Decade of Titus Livius (Ninian Hill Thomson trans., 1883)

cases.question: Why can a mixed constitution combining monarchy, aristocracy, and popular government be more stable than any pure form?
How could conflict between the Roman plebs and Senate make the republic freer and more powerful?
Is public liberty safer when guarded by ordinary people or by the nobles?
Why does a free republic need a lawful way for citizens to accuse powerful officials?
Why are public accusations useful but unsupported calumnies destructive to a republic?
Why must one person act alone when founding or completely reforming a republic?
How did Numa and Roman religion help establish laws, discipline, and civic order?
Why does a corrupt people have great difficulty preserving freedom after it is recovered?
Why is it shameful and dangerous for a prince or republic to lack armed forces of its own?
How did Rome's agrarian law create prolonged conflict and disorder within the republic?
Why is a crowd or multitude ineffective when it has no recognized leader?
In what sense can the people be wiser and more constant than a prince?
Did Rome acquire its empire chiefly through virtue or through fortune?
Why did the peoples who fought Rome defend their freedom so stubbornly?
What different methods can republics use to expand their power and territory?
Do men more often rise from humble beginnings to great fortune through fraud or through force?
What danger does a prince or republic face by relying on auxiliary or mercenary soldiers?
Why do truly powerful states obtain alliances through strength and reputation rather than buying friendships with money?
Why must a republic periodically return to its founding principles if it is to survive for a long time?
Why must a leader change his mode of action when circumstances and the times change?
Why should one commander rather than several leaders be placed over an army?
Why is anticipating the enemy's plans one of the highest qualities of a military commander?
How did prolonging military commands contribute to the loss of Roman liberty?
Why does Machiavelli consider fraud praiseworthy in the conduct of war?
